0.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she asked about the two rak'ahs after Asr and was told the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ never abandoned them
0.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia ditanya tentang dua rakaat setelah Ashar

1.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: "He is the worst of the three" — meaning the child of fornication when he follows the ways of both parents

10.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she asked: "Will a loved one remember his beloved on the Day of Resurrection?" The Messenger of Allah  ﷺ replied: "In three situations, no one will think of another..."
10.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia bertanya: "Apakah orang yang saling mencintai akan saling mengingat pada hari Kiamat?" Rasulullah  ﷺ menjawab: "Pada tiga keadaan, tidak ada yang akan memikirkan orang lain..."

11.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: "Pass me the prayer mat." She said she was menstruating. He replied: "Your menstruation is not in your hand"
11.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah  ﷺ bersabda: "Ambilkan aku tikar shalat." Ia berkata sedang haid. Beliau bersabda: "Haidmu bukan di tanganmu"
